q01.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q01.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q01.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Mira, a human disgraced soldier, level 22, out there somewhere)

q02.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q02.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q02.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q03.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q03.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q03.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q04.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q04.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q04.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q05.name: The Restless Crypt
q05.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q05.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q06.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q06.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q06.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q07.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q07.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q07.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q08.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q08.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q08.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q09.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q09.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q09.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q10.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q10.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q10.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q11.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q11.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q11.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q12.name: Webs in the Canopy
q12.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q12.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Leshanna
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q13.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q13.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q13.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q14.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q14.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q14.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q15.name: Rival Warband
q15.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q15.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q16.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q16.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q16.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Ghor, age 10)

q17.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q17.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q17.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q18.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q18.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q18.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Dizzy, a goblin rival adventurer, level 10, out there somewhere)

q19.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q19.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q19.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q20.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q20.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q20.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q21.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q21.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q21.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q22.name: Wolves at the Folds
q22.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q22.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q23.name: The Restless Crypt
q23.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q23.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q24.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q24.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q24.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q25.name: The Boiler-Cult
q25.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q25.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q26.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q26.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q26.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q27.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q27.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q27.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Ulric, age 8)

q28.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q28.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q28.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q29.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q30.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q30.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q31.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q32.name: The Boiler-Cult

q32.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q33.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q33.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q33.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q34.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q34.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q34.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q35.name: The Renegade Magus
q35.desc: A court wizard who stopped asking leave. The crown pays for the tower dark and the fires out.
q35.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q36.name: A Coffer Under Seal
q36.desc: A counting-house settles a debt abroad. The coffer is heavy, the road long, and the ledger says nothing else.
q36.epilogue: The coffer's weight is counted twice and found true; the party's name goes in the ledger under 'reliable'.

q37.name: The Sealed Dispatch
q37.desc: Court business under wax, and a rider who never arrived. Someone would rather it never does.
q37.epilogue: The seal is broken behind a shut door; whatever it says, couriers ride the same road twice as guarded within the week.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Edwin, a human duelist, level 3, out there somewhere)
